edges.description: Rare and structural variants in multiplex families are modeled as one genetic route into synaptic and neurodevelopmental process disruption.

edges.description: Genetic liability is modeled upstream of neuronal migration and neurite outgrowth pathways implicated by enrichment analyses.

edges.description: Synaptic process disruption is modeled upstream of circuit-level dysregulation in tic-generation pathways.

edges.description: Altered neuronal migration and outgrowth are represented as upstream developmental contributors to CSTC circuit vulnerability.

edges.description: Circuit dysregulation is modeled upstream of neurotransmitter-modulated motor and phonic tic output.
Altered neurotransmitter modulation of tic circuits produces recurrent motor and phonic tic behaviors.
Peripheral biomarker changes are represented as associated with TS biology but not as proven causal drivers of CSTC dysfunction.

nodes.description: Clonidine and guanfacine can be considered when Tourette syndrome coexists with attention deficit hyperactivity disorder.

evidence.reference_title: European clinical guidelines for Tourette syndrome and other tic disorders-version 2.0. Part III: pharmacological treatment
evidence.snippet: Other agents that can be considered include tiapride, risperidone, and especially in case of co-existing attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), clonidine and guanfacine.

nodes.description: Psychoeducation and behavioral approaches, including CBIT, habit-reversal training, and exposure-response prevention, are preferred first-line nonpharmacologic treatments when available and feasible.

evidence.reference_title: European clinical guidelines for Tourette syndrome and other tic disorders-version 2.0. Part III: pharmacological treatment
evidence.snippet: The first preference should be given to psychoeducation and to behavioral approaches, as it strengthens the patients’ self-regulatory control and thus his/her autonomy.

nodes.description: Tourette syndrome has familial clustering and complex genetic architecture involving common, rare, and structural variant contributions rather than a single-gene etiology for most cases.

evidence.snippet: Tourette syndrome (TS) is a neurodevelopmental disorder, manifested by tics and a variety of behavioral comorbidities that cluster strongly within families, suggesting a combination of genetic and environmental risk factors.

evidence.reference_title: Structural Variants and Implicated Processes Associated with Familial Tourette Syndrome
evidence.snippet: Gilles de la Tourette syndrome (GTS) is a neurodevelopmental psychiatric disorder with complex and elusive etiology with a significant role of genetic factors.

nodes.description: Tourette syndrome is modeled as involving abnormal regulation across cortical, striatal, and thalamic circuit nodes that participate in motor selection and tic suppression.

evidence.reference_title: Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation in Tourette Syndrome: A Historical Perspective, Its Current Use and the Influence of Comorbidities in Treatment Response.
evidence.snippet: In the pathophysiology of TS, cortico-striatal-thalamo-cortical circuits (CSTC) connecting brain cortical regions to basal ganglia, are critical to its presumed pathophysiology

evidence.reference_title: European clinical guidelines for Tourette syndrome and other tic disorders-version 2.0. Part III: pharmacological treatment
evidence.snippet: The largest amount of evidence supports the use of dopamine blocking agents, preferably aripiprazole because of a more favorable profile of adverse events than first- and second-generation antipsychotics.

nodes.description: Dopamine-sensitive neurotransmission is represented as a proximal modulator of motor and vocal tic expression, based on evidence for dopamine-blocking medication efficacy.

evidence.reference_title: European clinical guidelines for Tourette syndrome and other tic disorders-version 2.0. Part III: pharmacological treatment
evidence.snippet: The largest amount of evidence supports the use of dopamine blocking agents, preferably aripiprazole because of a more favorable profile of adverse events than first- and second-generation antipsychotics.

nodes.description: Dopamine-blocking pharmacotherapy, preferably aripiprazole, is used when behavioral approaches are ineffective, unavailable, or infeasible.

nodes.description: Multiple motor tics and at least one vocal or phonic tic define Tourette syndrome. Motor and phonic tics are the core features of TS, displaying a peculiar variability over time influenced by contextual factors.

evidence.snippet: The results further support the involvement of the regulation of neurotransmission, neuronal migration, and sound-sensing in GTS.

nodes.description: Peripheral immune, metabolic, and neurotrophic biomarkers differ between TS and control groups in meta-analysis, but larger standardized studies are needed before diagnostic use.

evidence.reference_title: Biomarkers and Tourette syndrome: a systematic review and meta-analysis
evidence.snippet: A total of 81 studies were identified, out of which 60 met the eligibility criteria for inclusion in the meta-analysis.

evidence.reference_title: Biomarkers and Tourette syndrome: a systematic review and meta-analysis
evidence.snippet: 13 comparisons were statistically significant [CD3+ T cell, CD4+ T cell, CD4+ T cell to CD8+ T cell ratio, NK-cell, anti-streptolysin O antibodies, anti-DNase antibodies, glutamic acid (Glu), aspartic acid (Asp), ferritin (Fe), zinc (Zn), lead (Pb), vitamin D, and brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF)].

nodes.description: Familial whole-genome sequencing identifies rare structural variants whose candidate gene sets are enriched for synaptic vesicle endocytosis and neurotransmission-related processes.
nodes.id: Structural Variant Effects on Synaptic Processes

evidence.reference_title: Structural Variants and Implicated Processes Associated with Familial Tourette Syndrome
evidence.snippet: Seventy putative pathogenic variants shared among affected individuals within one family but not present in the control group were identified.

evidence.reference_title: Structural Variants and Implicated Processes Associated with Familial Tourette Syndrome
evidence.snippet: Enrichment analysis showed that identified structural variants affected synaptic vesicle endocytosis, cell leading-edge organization, and signaling for neurite outgrowth.
